message:
feat: Add PowerShell installer for ntm on Windows (bd-1jt.4.5)

Add install.ps1 with full feature parity to install.sh:
- Version selection (-Version 1.2.3)
- Custom install prefix (-Prefix)
- Cache/offline mode (-CacheDir, -Offline)
- Checksum verification (SHA256)
- Minisign signature verification when available
- Non-interactive mode (-Yes or $env:CI=1)
- JSON output for automation (-Json)
- Verbose logging (-Verbose)

Installs to %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\ntm\bin by default (no admin required).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.4","title":"Generate SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) for releases","description":"# Generate SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) for Releases

## Purpose
Produce SBOMs for each release to improve supply-chain transparency.

## Formats
- SPDX JSON
- CycloneDX JSON

## Behavior
- Generate SBOMs after build, before release
- Attach as release assets
- For container images, attach SBOM attestation if enabled

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Uploaded to GitHub release
- [ ] Works for Rust/Go/Bun projects","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:28:31.491498168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T01:06:24.651217511Z","closed_at":"2026-01-31T01:06:24.651200039Z","close_reason":"Implemented src/sbom.sh module and dsr sbom command for SBOM generation using syft","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:47.963689719Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:28:31.491498168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.5","title":"Implement checksum auto-sync across flywheel repos","description":"# Checksum Auto-Sync Across Flywheel Repos

## Purpose
Update checksum manifests in downstream repos when installers change.

## Behavior
- Triggered after dsr release
- Recalculate checksums, update target repo in /tmp (never `/data/projects`)
- Use `git -C <repo>` for all git operations
- For external tools, open a security review issue instead of auto-merge

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Checksums recalculated after each release
- [ ] External changes flagged for review
- [ ] JSON output for verification","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:28:44.275881995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:53:30.421759326Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:28:44.275881995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:48.145176916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.6","title":"Implement SLSA provenance attestation for supply chain security","description":"# Implement SLSA Provenance Attestation

## Purpose
Generate SLSA v1 provenance (in-toto Statement + predicate) for release artifacts.

## Required Fields
- `_type`: in-toto Statement v1
- `predicateType`: https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1
- `subject[]`: artifact name + sha256 digest
- `predicate.buildDefinition`: buildType + externalParameters
- `predicate.runDetails`: builder id + timestamps + invocation id

## Behavior
- Generate provenance for each release (one per tool/version)
- Attach as release asset
- For containers, attach cosign attestation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Provenance generated for each artifact set
- [ ] Attached to GitHub release
- [ ] Verifiable with standard SLSA tooling","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:29:38.736005543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T01:10:27.951453988Z","closed_at":"2026-01-31T01:10:27.951436234Z","close_reason":"Implemented SLSA v1 provenance attestation in src/slsa.sh with slsa_generate(), slsa_generate_batch(), slsa_verify(), and slsa_generate_json() functions.  {"id":"bd-1jt.3.8","title":"Build multi-arch Docker images for containerized tools","description":"# Multi-Arch Docker Images

## Purpose
Build and publish multi-architecture Docker images (amd64/arm64) for tools that support containerization. Mirrors ntm release.yml docker job.

## Which Tools Get Containers
Not all tools need Docker images, but these do:
- ubs (Ultimate Bug Scanner) - CI/CD integration
- mcp_agent_mail - Server deployment
- process_triage (pt) - System administration

## Implementation
```bash
build_docker() {
  local tool="$1"
  local version="$2"
  local registry="ghcr.io/dicklesworthstone"
  local repo_dir="/data/projects/$tool"

  # Check for Dockerfile
  if [ ! -f "$repo_dir/Dockerfile" ]; then
    log "No Dockerfile for $tool, skipping container build"
    return 0
  fi

  # Build multi-arch with buildx (avoid global cd)
  docker buildx build     --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64     --tag "$registry/$tool:$version"     --tag "$registry/$tool:latest"     --push     --provenance=true     --sbom=true     -f "$repo_dir/Dockerfile" "$repo_dir"
}
```

## Container Signing
```bash
sign_container() {
  local image="$1"

  # Keyless signing with Cosign
  cosign sign --yes "$image"

  # Attach SBOM
  syft "$image" -o spdx-json | cosign attest --yes --predicate - --type spdx "$image"
}
```

## dsr Command
```bash
dsr docker build <tool>           # Build and push
dsr docker build <tool> --local   # Build only, don't push
dsr docker sign <image>           # Sign with cosign
```

## Prerequisites on trj
- Docker with buildx
- GHCR login (echo $GITHUB_TOKEN | docker login ghcr.io -u $USER --password-stdin)
- Cosign installed

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Multi-arch images build correctly
- [ ] Images pushed to GHCR
- [ ] Cosign signatures attached
- [ ] SBOM attestations included","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:36.001507944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:53:28.067445469Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:36.001507944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:49.477466240Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.9","title":"Implement minisign key management and secure storage","description":"# Minisign Key Management and Secure Storage

## Purpose
Create and protect the Minisign keypair used to sign release artifacts.

## Key Generation (Minisign)
```bash
minisign -G -p minisign.pub -s ~/.config/dsr/secrets/minisign.key
```
- Private key must be **password-protected**
- Public key is embedded in installers and docs

## Storage Policy
- Private key: `~/.config/dsr/secrets/minisign.key` (chmod 600)
- Public key: `~/.config/dsr/minisign.pub` + published in README
- Never store unencrypted key in repo or CI logs

## Distribution Strategy
Recommended: **central signing** on trj after artifact collection. Avoid copying private keys to multiple hosts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Keypair generated with password prompt
- [ ] Public key documented and embedded
- [ ] Private key stored with strict permissions
- [ ] Signing workflow uses central host only

## References
- https://jedisct1.github.io/minisign/","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:23:38.864145251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T16:09:48.606073123Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T16:09:48.606007460Z","close_reason":"Implemented minisign key management in src/signing.sh with functions for key generation (signing_init), verification (signing_check), artifact signing (signing_sign), and batch operations (signing_sign_batch). Added signing subcommand to dsr CLI. Keys stored in ~/.config/dsr/secrets/ with chmod 600 permissions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:23:38.864145251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.4","title":"Theme 4: Smart Curl-Bash Installers","description":"# Smart Curl-Bash Installers

## Purpose
Create intelligent install scripts for each tool that:
1. Try to download latest release binary first (fastest)
2. Detect if release is stale (>10 commits behind HEAD)
3. Offer to build from source if stale or binary unavailable
4. Auto-install required toolchains if user consents

## Installer URL Pattern
Each tool gets a hosted installer:
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/{tool}/main/install.sh
- Or via short URL: curl -sSL https://install.{tool}.dev | bash

## Usage
```bash
# Quick install (tries binary first)
curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/main/install.sh | bash

# Force source build
curl -sSL .../install.sh | bash -s -- --source

# Install specific version
curl -sSL .../install.sh | bash -s -- --version v1.5.0

# Install to custom location
curl -sSL .../install.sh | bash -s -- --prefix ~/.local

# Non-interactive (CI mode)
curl -sSL .../install.sh | bash -s -- --yes
```

## Installer Logic Flow
```
1. Detect OS and Architecture
   └─→ Linux x86_64, Linux ARM64, macOS ARM64, macOS x86_64, Windows

2. Check Latest Release
   └─→ gh api repos/Dicklesworthstone/{tool}/releases/latest (or curl fallback)

3. Compare Release Freshness
   └─→ Count commits between release tag and HEAD
   └─→ If >10 commits behind, release is "stale"

4. Decision Point
   ├─→ Release exists AND fresh (<10 commits behind)?
   │   └─→ Download binary → Install → Done
   │
   └─→ Release missing OR stale?
       └─→ Prompt: "Build from source? [y/N]"
           ├─→ No  → Install stale release anyway (with warning)
           └─→ Yes → Check toolchain → Build → Install
```

## Freshness Check Implementation (Best Practices)
- Prefer GitHub compare API via `gh_api` wrapper (JSON only)
- If repo already exists locally, use git plumbing: `git rev-list --left-right --count <tag>...<branch>`
- Never parse human output from `git status` or `gh run list`

## Toolchain Auto-Installation
When building from source, check and offer to install missing toolchains:

### Rust
```bash
if ! command -v cargo &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Rust toolchain not found."
  echo "Install Rust nightly? [y/N]"
  read -r response
  if [[ "$response" =~ ^[Yy] ]]; then
    curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- -y --default-toolchain nightly
    source "$HOME/.cargo/env"
  else
    die "Rust required for source build"
  fi
fi
```

### Go
```bash
if ! command -v go &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Go toolchain not found."
  echo "Install latest Go? [y/N]"
  read -r response
  if [[ "$response" =~ ^[Yy] ]]; then
    # Detect OS/arch and download appropriate binary
    GO_VERSION="1.23.0"
    GO_OS=$(uname -s | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')
    GO_ARCH=$(uname -m | sed 's/x86_64/amd64/;s/aarch64/arm64/')
    curl -sSL "https://go.dev/dl/go${GO_VERSION}.${GO_OS}-${GO_ARCH}.tar.gz" | sudo tar -C /usr/local -xzf -
    export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
  fi
fi
```

### Bun
```bash
if ! command -v bun &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Bun not found."
  echo "Install Bun? [y/N]"
  read -r response
  if [[ "$response" =~ ^[Yy] ]]; then
    curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash
    source "$HOME/.bun/bin/bun"
  fi
fi
```

## Cross-Platform Considerations

### macOS
- Use curl (built-in) not wget
- Install to ~/.local/bin (user-writable) by default
- Handle Gatekeeper for unsigned binaries (xattr -d com.apple.quarantine)

### Linux
- Check for sudo if installing to /usr/local/bin
- Support both apt and rpm-based distros for dependencies
- Handle SELinux contexts if necessary

### Windows (WSL or Git Bash)
- Detect WSL vs native Windows
- Use .exe extension for binaries
- Handle PATH differences

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Works on Linux x86_64, ARM64
- [ ] Works on macOS ARM64, x86_64
- [ ] Works on Windows (WSL at minimum)
- [ ] Correctly detects stale releases
- [ ] Toolchain installation is non-destructive
- [ ] --yes flag for CI/automation
- [ ] Detailed logging with --verbose
- [ ] Graceful failure with clear error messages

## Security Considerations
- Verify checksums after download
- Use HTTPS exclusively
- Don't execute arbitrary code from failed downloads
- Warn users about piping to bash","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:54.174177755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:35:43.089236552Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:54.174177755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.4.1","title":"Implement install.sh template generator","description":"# Implement install.sh Template Generator

## Purpose
Generate per-tool install scripts from a shared template while enforcing consistent safety, logging, and verification rules.

## Template Requirements
- Shebang: `#!/usr/bin/env bash`
- `set -uo pipefail` (no `set -e`)
- Explicit error handling for download/build/verify steps
- stderr for human logs, stdout for JSON/paths if `--json`

## Inputs (from repos.d)
- `repos.d/<tool>.yaml` supplies: tool name, repo, language, build_cmd, binary_name
- targets, artifact naming, archive format
- pre-release checks (if used by installer)

## Output
Generated scripts to `./installers/{tool}/install.sh`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Placeholders substituted correctly
- [ ] Generated scripts pass ShellCheck (warning+)
- [ ] Binary download + checksum + optional minisign verification works
- [ ] Cache/offline mode supported (if enabled)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:10:20.508569888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:10:47.582771008Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:10:47.582738517Z","close_reason":"Completed: Created src/install_gen.sh with install_gen_create, install_gen_all, install_gen_validate. Template generates ShellCheck-passing install.sh scripts with platform detection, checksum verification, offline support.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:02.476070991Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:22.919659290Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:10:20.508569888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:18:14.062990057Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:38:54.094130972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:39:45.431689318Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.4.2","title":"Implement toolchain version detection and auto-installation in installers","description":"# Toolchain Version Detection and Auto-Installation

## Purpose
Detect missing/outdated toolchains and offer to install them **without** breaking existing setups.

## Rules
- Never overwrite existing toolchains without explicit user consent
- Never auto-delete existing installs (print instructions instead)
- Respect `--non-interactive` (fail with actionable message)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects existing installations correctly
- [ ] Version checks work on all platforms
- [ ] Installation does not break existing setups
- [ ] Clear manual upgrade instructions provided","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:18:07.405296669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:07:18.386321869Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:07:18.386298995Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added portable pure-bash version comparison (no sort -V), jq/curl dependency checks. All detection functions working, safety rules enforced.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:18:07.405296669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.4.3","title":"Add minisign verification to install.sh (optional but recommended)","description":"# Add Minisign Verification to install.sh (Optional but Recommended)

## Purpose
Verify downloaded binaries with minisign signatures to prevent tampering.

## Behavior
- If minisign available: verify by default
- If missing: warn, optionally install, or fail when --require-signatures
- Embed public key in installer (from dsr config)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] install.sh verifies .minisig when available
- [ ] Clear failure if signature invalid
- [ ] Non-interactive mode supports --require-signatures","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:11.776660104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:13:27.576956969Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:13:27.576911403Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added _verify_minisign() function to install.sh template. Supports --require-signatures flag, embeds public key from config, downloads .minisig files, verifies with minisign tool.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:57.206972391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:57.459042194Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:11.776660104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:57.697458578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.4.4","title":"Add installer cache/offline mode + gh release download fallback","description":"# Add Installer Cache/Offline Mode + gh release download Fallback

## Purpose
Improve reliability when network is flaky by caching release assets and allowing offline installs.

## Behavior
- Cache downloads in ~/.cache/dsr/installers/<tool>/<version>
- Support --cache-dir and --offline flags
- Prefer gh release download when gh is available (auth + retries)
- Fallback to curl when gh is missing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cached assets reused when available
- [ ] Offline mode fails fast with clear error if cache missing
- [ ] gh release download works for private repos","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:19.727170447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:29:19.781146281Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:29:19.781128417Z","close_reason":"Implemented cache directory support (~/.cache/dsr/installers/<tool>/<version>), --cache-dir and --offline flags, auto-caching on download, and gh release download fallback for private repos. Added test_install_gen.sh with 9 passing tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:02.979791158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:19.727170447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:40:04.030679358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.4.5","title":"Provide PowerShell installer wrapper for native Windows","description":"# Provide PowerShell Installer Wrapper for Native Windows

## Purpose
Offer a first-class Windows install path (install.ps1) that mirrors install.sh behavior for users not using WSL.

## Behavior
- Detect OS/arch, download matching .exe
- Verify SHA256 (and minisign if available)
- Install to user-writable PATH (e.g., %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\dsr\bin)
- Support `-Yes`, `-Verbose`, `-Version`, `-Prefix`, `-CacheDir`, `-Offline`
- Respect non-interactive mode (`$env:CI=1` or `-Yes`)

## Logging
- Human-readable logs to stderr
- Optional JSON output for automation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] install.ps1 parity with install.sh (flags, logs)
- [ ] Works on Windows 10/11 without admin
- [ ] Signature verification supported when minisign present

+{"id":"bd-1jt.4.5","title":"Provide PowerShell installer wrapper for native Windows","description":"# Provide PowerShell Installer Wrapper for Native Windows

## Purpose
Offer a first-class Windows install path (install.ps1) that mirrors install.sh behavior for users not using WSL.

## Behavior
- Detect OS/arch, download matching .exe
- Verify SHA256 (and minisign if available)
- Install to user-writable PATH (e.g., %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\dsr\bin)
- Support `-Yes`, `-Verbose`, `-Version`, `-Prefix`, `-CacheDir`, `-Offline`
- Respect non-interactive mode (`$env:CI=1` or `-Yes`)

## Logging
- Human-readable logs to stderr
- Optional JSON output for automation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] install.ps1 parity with install.sh (flags, logs)
- [ ] Works on Windows 10/11 without admin
- [ ] Signature verification supported when minisign present
- [ ] Documented usage in README","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:25.838673495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T01:25:31.656429951Z","closed_at":"2026-01-31T01:25:31.656411156Z","close_reason":"Implemented PowerShell installer with all features: version selection, cache/offline mode, checksum and minisign verification, non-interactive mode, JSON output","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:03.227926195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:25.838673495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:40:08.359019185Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:40:08.618811604Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:40:08.857268243Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.5","title":"Theme 5: Testing & Validation","description":"# Testing & Validation

## Purpose
Ensure all components of the fallback release system work correctly through comprehensive unit, integration, and E2E tests with detailed logging.

## Testing Strategy

### 1. Unit Tests
- Platform detection
- Release freshness calculation
- Checksum/signature verification
- Toolchain detection and guardrails

### 2. Integration Tests
- Check → Build → Release pipeline (mocked GH/SSH)
- Repos registry management
- Status/report + prune dry-run

### 3. Contract Tests
- JSON schema validation for **every** `--json` output
- Stream separation: stderr empty on success

### 4. E2E Tests
- Docker containers for Linux installers
- Act-based fallback pipeline (mocked release)
- Package manager update workflow (Homebrew/Scoop) in dry-run

## Test Framework
Use bats-core for Bash tests.

## Logging Requirements
All tests must capture and print logs on failure (shared harness).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit + integration tests run <5 minutes
- [ ] E2E tests run <30 minutes
- [ ] JSON schema + stream separation validated
- [ ] Detailed logs available for failures","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:10:44.485219623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T01:22:09.098954383Z","closed_at":"2026-01-31T01:22:09.098936489Z","close_reason":"Theme 5: Testing & Validation complete. All sub-beads closed: unit tests, integration tests, E2E tests, canary testing, upgrade verification, and supply chain security tests.  {"id":"bd-1jt.5.1","title":"Write unit tests for platform detection and freshness checking","description":"# Unit Tests: Platform Detection & Freshness Checking

## Test File
tests/unit/test_core_functions.bats

## Platform Detection Tests

### Test Cases
\`\`\`bash
@test "detect Linux x86_64" {
  mock_uname "Linux" "x86_64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_equal "\$PLATFORM" "linux-amd64"
}

@test "detect Linux ARM64" {
  mock_uname "Linux" "aarch64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_equal "\$PLATFORM" "linux-arm64"
}

@test "detect macOS ARM64 (Apple Silicon)" {
  mock_uname "Darwin" "arm64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_equal "\$PLATFORM" "darwin-arm64"
}

@test "detect macOS x86_64 (Intel)" {
  mock_uname "Darwin" "x86_64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_equal "\$PLATFORM" "darwin-amd64"
}

@test "detect Windows via MINGW" {
  mock_uname "MINGW64_NT-10.0" "x86_64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_equal "\$PLATFORM" "windows-amd64"
}

@test "unsupported OS fails gracefully" {
  mock_uname "FreeBSD" "amd64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "Unsupported OS"
}

@test "unsupported arch fails gracefully" {
  mock_uname "Linux" "riscv64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "Unsupported architecture"
}
\`\`\`

## Freshness Check Tests

### Test Cases
\`\`\`bash
@test "release 0 commits behind is fresh" {
  mock_gh_compare 0
  run check_freshness "v1.0.0"
  assert_success
}

@test "release 10 commits behind is fresh (boundary)" {
  mock_gh_compare 10
  run check_freshness "v1.0.0"
  assert_success
}

@test "release 11 commits behind is stale (boundary)" {
  mock_gh_compare 11
  run check_freshness "v1.0.0"
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "commits behind"
}

@test "release 100 commits behind is very stale" {
  mock_gh_compare 100
  run check_freshness "v1.0.0"
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "100 commits behind"
}

@test "freshness check handles API error" {
  mock_gh_error "rate limit exceeded"
  run check_freshness "v1.0.0"
  # Should warn but not fail hard
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "Could not check freshness"
}

@test "freshness check without gh CLI uses curl fallback" {
  unset_command "gh"
  mock_curl_compare 5
  run check_freshness "v1.0.0"
  assert_success
}
\`\`\`

## Mock Helpers
\`\`\`bash
# tests/helpers/mocks.bash

mock_uname() {
  local os="\$1" arch="\$2"
  function uname() {
    case "\$1" in
      -s) echo "\$os" ;;
      -m) echo "\$arch" ;;
    esac
  }
  export -f uname
}

mock_gh_compare() {
  local ahead_by="\$1"
  function gh() {
    if [[ "\$*" == *"compare"* ]]; then
      echo "{\"ahead_by\": \$ahead_by}"
    fi
  }
  export -f gh
}

mock_gh_error() {
  local error="\$1"
  function gh() {
    echo "gh: \$error" >&2
    return 1
  }
  export -f gh
}
\`\`\`

## Logging Output
Each test logs:
- Input parameters
- Mock configuration
- Function output
- Assertions checked

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All platform combinations tested
- [ ] Boundary conditions for freshness tested
- [ ] Error cases handled
- [ ] Mock helpers reusable
- [ ] Tests run in <10 seconds","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:11:32.707115669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:34.630160141Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:34.630141697Z","close_reason":"Unit tests for platform detection and freshness checking implemented in tests/unit/test_core_functions.bats - all 29 tests pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:15:53.451303881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:11:32.707115669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:53:11.550295352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.10","title":"Write tests for config loader + doctor + GitHub API adapter","description":"# Tests for Config Loader + Doctor + GitHub API Adapter

## Coverage
- Config precedence (flags > env > file > defaults)
- Schema validation failures
- Doctor detection for missing deps
- API adapter caching (ETag) + rate-limit handling
- **Runtime guardrails**: absolute path normalization + safe delete helpers
- **Bash version gating** behavior (non-interactive error paths)
- **Logging layout**: date-based log dirs + `logs/latest` symlink

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit tests for config resolution
- [ ] Integration tests for doctor outputs
- [ ] Mocked API adapter tests with cache hits/misses
- [ ] Guardrail helpers reject relative paths
- [ ] Safe delete refuses non-dsr paths
- [ ] Logging layout matches XDG spec","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:37.282238775Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T21:56:00.040915982Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T21:56:00.040772292Z","close_reason":"Added XDG layout tests to test_logging.sh (date-based dirs, latest symlink).
